region_of_origin: Global — bone-in skin-on chicken thigh is the most common chicken cut sold at Singapore wet markets
food_subgroup: Raw poultry — moderately fatty dark meat; 100g raw bone-in skin-on reference; moderate fat (~11.5g per 100g as sold)

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw chicken thigh as a food product in available reference; left null rather than invent a code.

serving.natural_language_portion: One average raw chicken thigh as sold at a Singapore wet market weighs approximately 115g bone-in skin-on, yielding roughly 55–60g of edible cooked meat after cooking and bone removal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Thigh, Skin-on, Bone-in, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 172849)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172849: Chicken, thigh, bone-in, with skin, raw. All per-100g nutrient values taken directly from this record. No adaptation required.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw chicken thigh contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g (USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI testing is performed on zero-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (82mg per 100g raw)
hypertension.guidance: Raw chicken thigh contains only 82mg sodium per 100g, well below the threshold of concern for hypertension; it is a suitable protein choice provided it is prepared without added salt, sauces, or marinades that would substantially increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0g carbohydrate and a glycemic index that is not applicable, raw chicken thigh produces no postprandial glucose rise; the moderate fat content (11.5g/100g) may slightly slow gastric emptying when consumed alongside carbohydrate-containing foods, providing modest additional glucose moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (3.2g/100g) and cholesterol (82mg/100g); MUFA-dominant fat profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Chicken thigh contains 3.2g saturated fat and 82mg cholesterol per 100g bone-in weight; removing the skin reduces saturated fat by approximately 6–7g per 100g edible portion, and the MUFA-dominant fatty acid profile (oleic acid) is considered cardioprotective when consumed in the context of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: MUFA-dominant fat profile; zinc content supports immune function
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The oleic-acid-dominant fat profile of chicken dark meat is considered anti-inflammatory, and the zinc content (2mg/100g bone-in, approximately 3.6mg per 100g edible meat) supports immune regulation relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Thigh (Raw, Skin-on, Bone-in) is the upper-leg cut of the chicken sold with skin and bone intact, as commonly found at Singapore wet markets. Per 100g raw bone-in weight (USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849): 167 kcal, 15.4g protein, 11.5g total fat (3.2g saturated, 4.49g monounsaturated, 2.49g polyunsaturated), 0g carbohydrate, 82mg sodium, 248mg potassium, 82mg cholesterol, 163mg phosphorus, 2mg zinc, 18.4mcg selenium. The fat content is approximately four times higher than skinless chicken breast. Cooking yield: 100g raw bone-in yields approximately 55g cooked edible meat after bone removal and moisture loss (roughly 45% loss). Tracking alert: calorie-tracking apps typically use boneless edible-portion values — users weighing bone-in raw chicken should halve the reported weight to approximate the edible cooked portion. Removing the skin before cooking saves approximately 6–7g fat per 100g. Glycemic index is not applicable (0g carbohydrate). The MUFA-dominant fatty acid profile (oleic acid) is considered anti-inflammatory. Suitable for halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849. Confidence: very high.
common_user_queries: how many calories in a chicken thigh
meal_context: raw ingredient for stir-fry, braising, grilling, or roasting

confidence_reason: All nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172849 (Chicken, thigh, bone-in, with skin, raw), an approved primary source with exact match to the food described. No estimation or adaptation was required for core macros or secondary nutrients.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849 — raw chicken thigh contains no carbohydrate; this is naturally zero for this animal protein. Sugar_g and dietary_fibre_g are also naturally zero for this food. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA source). Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source). Glycemic index is set to null and gi_category to 'not_applicable' because this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g per 100g); GI testing is not performed on zero-carbohydrate foods. Water_g (70.7g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849. Monounsaturated_fat_g (4.49g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.49g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 172849 fatty acid breakdown. TRACKING NOTE: This record represents 100g raw bone-in skin-on weight as sold. The edible cooked portion is approximately 55g per 100g raw bone-in. Users should account for bone and skin weight when logging. No SNOMED CT code identified; left null to preserve ontology honesty.
